sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports experiencing pain following a soccer injury, describing a fall resulting in foot pain. He has a history of soccer-related injuries but none previously affecting this foot. Since the injury, he has been elevating the foot, icing it, and taking ibuprofen. He rates his pain as a 3 without ibuprofen and a 1 with ibuprofen. The patient reports no numbness or tingling in his left foot.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals bruising and swelling on the left foot, tenderness to palpation, and a positive ankle-plantar test. Capillary refill is less than 3 seconds. Dorsalis pedis pulse is strong and intact. Motor and sensory function are intact. The x-ray demonstrates a dorsal displacement of the second metatarsal base with a 3mm separation of the first and second metatarsal bases and a bony fragment in the Lisfranc joint space.
medical_concepts: dorsal displacement of the second metatarsal base
separation of the first and second metatarsal bases
bony fragment in the Lisfranc joint space

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has a Lisfranc fracture, likely requiring surgical intervention to stabilize the bones and ligaments in the foot. The injury poses a risk of long-term complications such as arch loss and arthritis if not addressed surgically.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: An MRI will be ordered to assess ligament injuries. The patient will undergo surgery to repair the Lisfranc fracture with plates and screws. A cast and crutches will be provided. Weight-bearing will be restricted for 6-8 weeks, followed by gradual progression based on tolerance. Follow-up will be scheduled in the clinic in one week.

medical_concepts: dorsal displacement of the second metatarsal base
separation of the first and second metatarsal bases
bony fragment in the Lisfranc joint space
